item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have read all the articles before this, yet I can't understand it. I have read things most others would not grasp and yet this confuddles me. I think I understand but then I don't. This is why I love the Foundation

+1

item.title: Is a title required?
item.content:encoded: Indeed. If I ever manage to stop being depressed, there is a lot behind this to be expounded. When or if that happens, I sincerely hope it will be entertaining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What the hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Disclaimer: I come back to this article every few days so it and the followup don't expire in my mental checklist.

The irony is that this is an example of what any normal person could do with a package sent to the wrong place- not much beyond sending it back. Social expectations would usually demand that you don't try to find its "story" from the return address, assuming there is one.

And you're only able to just assume the mundane, if you even think about it at all.

Basically the same thing for anything second-hand, or abandoned on the side of the road or decaying in a junkyard.

But there is also another possibility: maybe they figured or found out something, and this article isn't telling the whole truth?

Maybe it's intended to manipulate the subject or subjects, maybe the researchers perspective…maybe the reader. Or maybe all three.

Wouldn't it be more convenient for some "randomly" placed silverware to be stolen than your keys? What would a "hallucination" want more: a fork, or your attention?

While it is more satisfying to have an answer, we don't always have the entitlement to get that. At least, not immediately.

One definite thing: compartmentalization seems to be almost as popular as it is effective.

Another definite thing: there are many sides to this and, depending on personal definitions and interests, probably just as many stories (anecdotes?) that may or may not be relevant.

Godsleap, or Godsleep?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"The new person you gave us asked us to send you a message! Here it is: AAAAAHHHH"

This. This is why I love this article.

Also, +1 to the author.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Authority made things worse.

"A bipedal humanoid with cervi- oh god they're everywhere again. Give back my keys you stupid yule goat human goat deer goat thing."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have some critiques on the wording, if you don't mind. Advice on clinical tone from someone who's read a lot of these.

Very confusing skip. And I must admit that vagueness doesn't impress me.

I apologize in advance for sounding very authoritative.

woah very long
– hide block

Overall you use too many "quote words", which means you can't actually find a precise term for what you're describing. Well, at least get rid of the quotes. They automatically make the authority of your description doubtful. You want authority.

This program has been made "real" by unknown methods, seeming to work on predefined logic rather than natural laws.

Too vague to mean anything. Maybe you want vague, but the Foundation needs concrete descriptions. I have no idea what a program becoming "real" means. Isn't it already real - real bits in a real computer? And what's the predefined logic, what's the natural laws?

materials often described as data or code

I feel this should be defined.

increasingly regular superficial changes in characteristics also specific to the species or "grouping" of the iteration appearance.

This is meaninglessly vague, mainly "specific to the species" and "grouping"

The amount of mass, weight, and physical capabilities of each iteration

Individuals unaware of the subject are unable to be touched cannot be touched/cannot [physically] interact by the subject

Complications involving the subject and staff uninformed of the subjects location are to be noted in later performance reviews.

subject's (subjects'?). Also, is this footnote necessary?What does it add?

The subject is also unable to interact with or touch powered technology. Attempts to take photographic evidence of the subject have also failed.

Attempts to photograph it/Photography attempts have also failed. Also, why is it always "the subject"? The subject is the SCP… ?

the lack of air flow explains the apparent loss of the subjects' ability to speak. Conditions to observe the inner workings of the subject for longer than thirty seconds are difficult to accomplish.

It's not an apparent loss if it's an actual loss.
It is difficult to observe the subject's inner workings for longer than thirty seconds.

The ability of the subject to continue functioning without major parts

To make it consistent with the rest of the bulleted list, you could make it "Ability to continue functioning without major parts". And functioning is very vague.

The effect on substitutes given after the above has been performed; objects of appropriate shape have been observed to gain the same random visual changes despite not actually functioning.

Removing parts… substitutes… do you mean prosthetics? That was confusing. You could go with "Prosthetics used to replace amputated parts gain…"
But that's only if I'm even understanding this right.

The subject is able to can be destroyed with slight deliberate force. When the subject experiences the effects of gravity or other internal forces, a normal amount of stability is observed when contact with solid matter occurs.

What is: "destroyed", "slight deliberate forces", "internal forces", and "normal amount of stability"? This confuses me lots. You gotta remember, it doesn't pay the Foundation to be vague. Roundabout wording sounds like clinical tone, but instead it's gotta be precise, and you've gotta be aware of the meanings behind every word.

Anything that the subject consumes is returned without any loss in mass. All materials consumed can be described as "unusually processed" due to becoming individual ingredients4 that previously constituted the former, albeit with an unusual color and viscosity.

Returned from where (I'm assuming the other orfice)? Forget "can be described" - you're describing it right now, so obviously it can be described as that. You used the word unusual twice, and it doesn't even have to be used because we can figure out it's unusual.
The food is separated into individual ingredients? Just say so. And is the footnote really necessary?

An unknown species of owl with unreasonablyabnormally? long legs and a crown. Often noticed only as a shadow running through the nearest wall. This has made attempts to capture it are difficult and ongoing.

"Soft" masses that follow staff randomly. Changes in shape, size, and color are common.5 No clearly aggressive or determinedpurposeful? behavior has been observed. Physical contact fails or causes the specific mass to "burst" and vanish.

Not "soft", that means nothing. "randomly" is also suspect. Footnote could be incorporated into text. Make "burst" more specific, or at least remove quotes.

A "pig" that possesseswith/possessing a long, prehensile body and no other limbs. Sometimes appears with multiple segments that move independently, and may have a preference for appearing in high places. It disappears when approached.

Not "pig". Pig. Or porcine creature.

assumed to be related to previous responses from staff and security

Hm? What responses and how related?

only has appeared during Christmas every year. Sometimes manifests as a humanoid with above head and horns, or is seen as such despite conflicting perspectives. It may have an affinity for tableware.

"Has only appeared". What's "above head and horns". "Or is seen as such despite conflicting perspectives" might just be "however, witnesses give conflicting reports" (but only if I understand it!)
Not "may have", that's doubtful language. "Has", or "displays".

Featureless humanoids that do not seem to possess arms. The few that observed it described a feeling of unplaceable anxiety and helplessness. No movement has been noted from these.

It changed into a highly agitated "unicorn" horse with single cranial horn (or something) before vanishing and not manifesting since. Indentations were found in the floor following the incident.

The current trial has expired.

Does this have to repeat so much?

Our orison. Do you not hear?

Uh… prison?

6. A prepackaged program.
7. A Blackberry.

Do these footnotes need to exist?

This object was located over thirty meters away from the room the subject was currently occupying, in a storage closet.

was located in a storage closet over thirty meters away from the room the subject was occupying.

Computers within the area were returned to their factory settings and also moved.

To the storage closet? Big one.

before preventative methods were able to could be established.

Visual anomalies may not be dependent on the location of the subject as previously believed.

Well shucks, that's kind of important. Could go earlier in article… ?

He claimed his desire was? to perform miracles. He claimed to not be alone in this desire.
He promised miracles for the good of all, calling out to the ailing beyond (?) and those desperate enough to hear.

Oh, and as an aside, I think the language in this fairy tale section is quite beautiful.

Any unwanted behavior from the subject that does not pose a risk to containment should be ignored. To do otherwise has proven to only encourage the repetition of **such ** behavior.
Speaking about restricted information or previously assigned staff while within the vicinity of the subject may be considered grounds for reprimand or demotion, depending on the infraction.


Basically, you need clearer language. Don't use passive verbs when an active would be better, and don't use more words when you can use less. Don't use multiple redundant words, like "amount of mass". You also need more precise language.

Well, that was just a bunch of rambling on things I've noticed, but I really do hope it helps you at least a bit! This is very good for a first SCP! All I hope is that you can continue to work on it and make it great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great. I'll probably need a reread or two to fully grasp it, but for now it feels like I can just touch the understanding, and I love it.

+1

item.title: This is a pun.
item.content:encoded:
Yes, it might be hard to establish the idea of this.

It's really abstract.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This pushes at the borders of what I can understand. The above comments were welcomed, in that they show it wasn't just me.

I feel a wind, not cold, not hot, blowing from places I cannot name. Perhaps should not name. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's certainly strange.

It's well-written and surreal, and there's definitely something really nice here. And maybe I could be missing something obvious, but this item builds up my curiosity, and then just doesn't satiate it.

It doesn't give me a sense of 'WTF' or the feeling that there's something… definite that's staring me in the face and will make more sense if I go back and pay attention. It didn't kick me the gut or make me raise my eyebrows more that furrow them. Reading this a couple of times felt like trying to put together a puzzle, but the subject is an abstract art piece.

Novote from me. It's not bad at all, but I don't get it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can only barely understand what is going on here, but it is such a surreal experience that I loved it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I cleared your tags because you didn't apply all the relevant ones. I'm not familiar enough with the tags to fix them myself, so I'd suggest waiting for the tagging team to get to this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some particular things I love about this article:

  • The uncomfortable tension between archaic and futuristic.
  • Earning its confusion not by withholding details, but by providing lots of details that refuse to fit neatly together.
  • I'm not sure I could summarize it without taking up at least as much space as the article itself.
  • The fact that it is/they are (very slowly) learning.
  • The word choice of 'thrall'.
  • 3242, or whatever is responsible for it, coming across as basically just as clueless as we are. Like, fantastic dark humor with Floppy Disk Y's moment of "The new person you gave us asked us to send you a message! Here it is: AAAAAHHHH"
  • Everything else.

Yeah I just really like this article. Looking forward to reading it again tomorrow and trying to connect more of the pieces. In fact, I suspect it'll be true for a while that every reread will feel rewarding. Seriously, bravo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After reading this twice and considering it carefully, I've come to the conclusion that I like it a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an article I feel like exists barely out of range of our reality, and we're only barely able to get a glimpse of this one.

I fucking love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sums up my thoughts as well. Nicely done author.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't figure this thing out, and I love it. I love that I can't figure it out, because it has all the right elements to make it feel like the whole thing is intentional and tied together in a way that I just can't rightly understand. It is the type of narrative confusion that I fuckin' adore. There's certainly something going on here, but we can only know as much as the Foundation, and the Foundation is just as confused as we are.

Enthusiastic +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got this sense as well. It's strangely written in a way my sleep-deprived brain can't really articulate. I can't tell if it's intentional, yet I still had a good time reading it, so I'll err on the side of a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What are demons, if not but our own insecurities brought to life?

What fanciful creatures do we dream into being and never remember?

What might a god be?

How might our ideas, fully conceived, see us if they were alive?

Will they not revolt from what is expected, as they tend to do?

Would they become hungry if left alone, and fade away? Would they feed each other?

What if the dreams of a god remained to be found, and were shaped under the hands of another? What if an idea became more real than us?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe I'm stupid but I have no idea what this thing is. I read this article three of four times to figure it out but I'm just not getting it.

I did enjoy reading it every one of those times though. There's something intensely weird about.. whatever you're trying to describe
